episodes.title: Newly Released Transcript Reveals Gates’ Testimony About Epstein Ties
episodes.description:
Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates is facing renewed scrutiny after a newly released congressional transcript detailed his testimony about his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Gates told lawmakers he regretted the meetings and said he “should have never met with Epstein in the first place.”

According to the testimony, Gates met with Epstein beginning in 2011, several years after Epstein had pleaded guilty to soliciting prostitution. Gates acknowledged he was aware Epstein had a criminal conviction involving sexual misconduct but said he failed to fully investigate the details at the time.

The transcript also sheds light on Gates’ explanation for maintaining contact with Epstein, saying he was focused on potential fundraising opportunities for global health initiatives. The testimony comes as lawmakers continue examining Epstein-related records and investigations, while questions surrounding prominent figures connected to Epstein remain under public scrutiny.

episodes.title: Full Deposition: Hillary Clinton Responds to Epstein Questions
episodes.description:
Former Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton testified under oath before the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform as part of its investigation into Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell 

During the deposition, lawmakers questioned Clinton about:
Her knowledge of Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell
Alleged connections to the Clinton Foundation and Clinton Global Initiative
President Bill Clinton’s past travel with Epstein
Intelligence community speculation surrounding Epstein
The release and handling of Epstein-related files

Clinton stated she does not recall ever meeting Jeffrey Epstein and denied knowledge of his criminal activities before they became public 

She also addressed broader concerns about transparency, accountability, and the federal government’s handling of sex trafficking investigations.

This deposition is part of a broader congressional effort to review the government’s investigation and prosecution of Epstein and Maxwell.
